entry.title: Re: Controling SMARTFORMS from SUBMIT statement
entry.content: This issue is not related to SUBMIT. You may append content to an existing Spool Request if this spool request is not "final" (search "final" or "do not append print jobs" in the print dialog/print settings), provided that you don't force the generation of a New Spool Request of course (search "new spool request" in the print dialog/print settings).

The action works fine, but there is a large amount of data available, so I am unable to get all the data. I am receiving a timeout error. We can provide inputs such as filtering for the top records, but I want to retrieve all the data. I have read about pagination in the SAP help documents, but I am a bit confused about how to implement it.

I am using this data in form creation and approval workflows for dropdown fields.

It would be greatly appreciated if anyone could help me with this.

Thank you!

entry.title: Migration Cockpit Usage for Loading New Assets
entry.content:
Hello,

 

We are seeking clarification regarding the capabilities of the SAP Migration Cockpit, specifically in the context of asset management. Our question is whether the Migration Cockpit can be utilized to load new assets into the system, as opposed to its typical use case of migrating legacy assets from a previous system.

 

We would appreciate guidance on the following points:

  1. Can the Migration Cockpit be configured or adapted for the purpose of uploading new assets directly?
  2. Are there any specific templates, configurations, or prerequisites required for this use case?
  3. If the Migration Cockpit is not intended for this scenario, could you recommend alternative tools or best practices within SAP for efficiently handling the mass upload of new assets?

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Migration Cockpit Usage for Loading New Assets
entry.content:
Migration Cockpit is intended to upload legacy assets.

For mass upload of new assets, please consider using several BAPIs, like BAPI_FIXEDASSET_CREATE et al.

entry.title: Re: How to use API pagination in build action project_SAP...
entry.content: To use API pagination in SAP Build Process Automation, set up an API call with pagination support. Use a loop to fetch data from each page until all results are retrieved. Store and process the data, checking for a NeXT Page or similar parameter to continue fetching. Use the http Request action to handle the API calls and manage pagination efficiently.

entry.title: Expose external API to SAP DM
entry.content:
HI, 
I am trying to test a scenario where: Expose an S/4HANA private cloud API to SAP Digital Manufacturing, and use this API as external API in the production process designer. 
My progress is as below: 

  1. Create destination on SAP BTP

    Checked connection: Green without any error. 
    User is technical user created in the ERP system. 
  2. Create server via app "Manage Web Servers" in SAP DM

    After server SCN_S4DEMO created, I connected this server to DMC_Cloud server. So now server SCN_S4DEMO will be consumed by DMC_Cloud. 
  3. Create service via app "Manage Service Registry" in SAP DM
     
    Added service definition here with response as well. 
  4. Create production process with this service via app "Production process designer" in SAP DM

    But this production process kept giving the same error at step 2: 

    If i look into the error message in debug mode, it says Failed to resolve "Host" (this info is identical to the URL stated at step 1). 

Seems like SAP DM cannot speak to the backend system but it is connected when click on Check Connection in BTP. 
Note: everything is configured based on https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-digital-manufacturing/business-process-extensions-developer-s-guide/api-services-restful-and-odata

Any help is appreciated! 

KR,
Sunny

entry.title: SAP DM - Discrete Orders - Sampling Requirements (different across process steps)
entry.content:
Hello, we are working on some discrete production with two different cases and wanted to see how the community has approached these in their sites and implementations in SAP Digital Manufacturing.

We've been exploring splitting and merging SFC's through production process to automate these activities. Our goal is to ensure sampling requirements for our data collections in the recipe are meet at the right cadence and ensuring a clean user interface (with splitting we've been worried about having hundreds of SFC's in the Work Center POD or Operation Activity POD -- process lots are something we've been exploring for this).

Would love to get advice/suggestions from the community on approaches! Thanks in advance!

Scenario A - single machine with different zones 

Step 1Check every 2500Zone 2
Step 2Check every 5000Zone 2
Step 3Check every 7500Zone 3
Step 4Varies based on qty per boxPackaging

Scenario B - three machines different zones and physical WIP between locations/processes

Step 1Check every 2500Machine 1
Step 2Check every 5000Machine 2
Step 3Check every 7500Machine 3

entry.title: Where can I learn how to set-up SAP S/4HANA Sales and configure the essential functions?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Configuring SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Sales learning journey.

Overview 
In this Learning journey consultants and project team members will learn how to perform configuration tasks to adapt the standard functions and settings of the SAP system to the company's business needs.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, application consultants will be able to set-up SAP S/4HANA Sales and configure the essential functions.

  • Setting up the Enterprise Structures in Sales
  • Configuring Business Partner and Customer Master Data in SAP S/4HANA Sales
  • Customizing of Sales Documents: Setting up of a Sales Document Type, Item Categories and Schedule Line Categories
  • Data Flow – Setting up the Copying Control in Sales
  • Partner Functions in Sales
  • Customizing output control and text control
  • Incomplete Documents (coming soon)
  • Outline Agreements (coming soon)

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Enable a Clean Core with SAP Cloud ALM- Webinar Summary
entry.content:
Enable a Clean Core with SAP Cloud ALM- Webinar Summary

Approach for this Blog

In this blog I have documented the summary from the Virtual Webinar Series on Enable a Clean Core with SAP Cloud ALM.

Summary of sessions:

  • Session 1 - Maximizing Clean Core Benefits with SAP Cloud ALM- 20th November 2024
  • Session 2 - Efficiently Managing RISE with SAP Projects: A Clean Core Approach Using SAP Cloud ALM-21st November 2024
  • Session 3 - Streamlining Business Processes While Preserving a Clean Core- 26th November 2024
  • Session 4 - Ensuring a Clean Core with SAP Cloud ALM: Test, Change, and Deployment Management- 28th November 2024
  • Session 5 - Operational Excellence with SAP Cloud ALM: Driving the Clean Core Initiative – 3rd December 2024
  • New & Planned offerings on the Cloud ALM Roadmap for clean core adoption
  • Planned Future Roadmap to enhance Cloud ALM capability

Session 1 - Maximizing Clean Core Benefits with SAP Cloud ALM- 20th November 2024

What is Clean Core?

Clean core is a journey, which needs to be adapted by following a step-by-step approach.

Greenfield

  • Unique opportunity to follow clean core principles right from beginning.
  • Establish strong governance.

Brownfield

  • Establish clean core principle for every new deployment.
  • Plan your journey towards a clean core.

Benefits of Clean Core

Case Study of Hitachi

Challenges

  • 9000 customization in legacy  SAP ERP application
  • Manage ERP version upgrade cycle.

Value Driven Results

  • Adopting fit to standard approach has allowed Hitachi to reduce core system customization from more than 9000 to 22.
  • Developments are now reused across the landscape.
  • A drastically less customized landscape has seen update cycle reduced from every five years to yearly.

The impact

  • 94% reduction in customization footprint
  • 18x handling of solution updates
  • 100% of on-site maintenance requirements eliminated

Business Transformation and clean core

 

How each dimension of Cloud ALM supports clean core

 

How project management can be used for clean core.

  • As clean core is a journey, it’s an ongoing process and cannot be adapted at once.
  • We need to make sure we are following the Best practices and adapting the SAP Activate methodologies to be agile compliant in our clean core journey.
  • Define teams and roles.
  • Define Timeboxes and releases.
  • Adapt Q-Gates Management
  • Overall dashboarding for tracking the project progress.

You can find the E2E project management details in my blog which was demonstrated in the webinar. https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/all-the-buzz-about-sap-cloud-alm/ba-p/13881779

Rise with SAP Methodology Dashboard

  • It fetches the data from multiple sources, Signavio, leanIx, Q-gates , readiness report etc.
  • Supports in making decisions which steps to be taken in what sequence for clean core adaption
  • Bridging the gap from analysis to execution by task creation and distribution among project team

Session 2 - Efficiently Managing RISE with SAP Projects: A Clean Core Approach Using SAP Cloud ALM-21st November 2024

What is Clean Core Functionality?

  • Contains tasks to learn more about clean core methodology
  • Clean core success plan is a subset of 118 tasks currently
  • Cleanup unused code
  • To be as close to standard

What is a Q-gate?

  • Predefined milestones where a project is audited to check, if it meets the necessary criteria move to next phase.
  • Checkpoints for the current defined project task

 What is the importance of Q-gates in clean core adaption?

To tack cross project clean core activities.

Automatically generated Q-gates in clean core adaption

 

Project Setup and roadmap selection for clean core adaption

There are three ways to manage clean core tasks

  • As a part of implementation project
  • As a part of system conversion
  • In a consolidation project

For all the variants Q-gates are automatically created as per the selected roadmap. Selecting a roadmap while creating a project, below are the available roadmaps in Cloud ALM for project setup.

 

Select a roadmap and SAVE, the Q-gates gets auto populated as below.

 

 

Click on the generated Q-gate to open and start working on the pre-defined checklist with available drop down options as below.

 

What is the clean core governance preparation process?

  • Create Tag group and tags

 

Below Tags were created under the tag group for clean core adaption as these are the Cloud ALM entities to establish clean core governance.

  • Process
  • Requirements
  • User Stories
  • Features

 

What is Solution Process Traceability and how it can be leveraged for clean core adaption?

To analyze the readiness of scoped solution processes based on the progress of related requirements and Test execution. We can find the answers to questions about the readiness of related entities. 

The Solution Process Traceability has been upgraded with a feature to do the indirect assignments as below:

New and planned offerings

  • To have an automated dashboard to visualize clean core related information. The dashboard will automatically populate the clean core dashboard based on the clean core related information from the below data providers:
  • Q-Gates
  • Solution Process
  • Requirements
  • User Stories
  • Features

Session 3 - Streamlining Business Processes While Preserving a Clean Core- 26th November 2024

How to transform business from strategy to execution?

Going from an ECC system to legacy S/4 Hana it's a continuous cycle, do a fit to standard workshop, document the findings and decided to redesign the process that needs to be implemented.

It’s a continuous process using SAP integrated tools.

  • SAP Signavio: SAP Signavio Process tools are primarily used for process analysis and process mining activities.
  • SAP Cloud ALM : It helps customers to implement and operate their cloud or hybrid solutions.
  • SAP Leanix: SAP LeanIX is a SaaS application that allows to manage and optimize enterprise architecture.
  • SAP Enable Now: SAP Enable Now is a digital adoption platform (DAP) from SAP that provides in-application help and e-learning content to improve the adoption, productivity, and efficiency of SAP software
  • Tricentis: Accelerates testing of all your applications, data, and business processes and integrates with tools across your delivery pipeline.

What is SAP Rise with SAP Methodology Framework?

  1. SAP Signavio is the starting point for modelling the business process.
  2. Landscape discovery involves documenting the organization's IT landscape, planning, designing, and overseeing the implementation of architectural changes to meet changing business needs.
  3. Create a project on Cloud ALM with required roadmap selection
  4. Conduct fit-to-standard workshops, to identify the gaps.
  5. Project execution begins in the realize phase with SAP Activate methodology.

 

E2E Process flow of SAP Rise with SAP Methodology Framework

 

Insights of the Clean core adaption for Business Process Transformation

  • Define Solution process and create requirements with user story and subtasks.
  • Adapt best practice solution process or customize if needed.
  • Copy solution process flow diagram into a custom process
  • Adapt Business Process accordingly.

 

Session 4 - Ensuring a Clean Core with SAP Cloud ALM: Test, Change, and Deployment Management- 28th November 2024

Cloud ALM Test management capabilities

  • Test management in cloud alm can be manual or automatic through Tricentis. Test automation API’s provide liberty to integrate further 3rd party tools.
  • Test case is associated to Solution Process scope & mapped to a requirement for test Planning.
  • The test case can be built and executed manually or automatic through Tricentis.
  • A Defect can be raised through cloud alm defect Management to identify the gaps in the test results.

How  Tricentis is used to build and execute the test cases?

  • Module based test automation in the cloud.
  • Use XScan to scan screen and relevant UI elements
  • Use Builder to assemble the Test Case based Scanned Modules, Reusable test steps and SAP standard module.

 

 

Analyze the test results in a central entry for all test related information and activities in the dashboard.

 

Current change management capability of cloud alm

What is a Feature?

  • Functionality that is represented by a feature must be deployed to a target system together
  • Create a feature and assign a transport to be deployed in target systems.

 

 

  • To restrict the manual TR creation and release on development system, as per the SAP recommendation it should be done through authorizations on the managed system.
  • Mass deployment of transports to quality at the moment is only possible through the STMS import job. The deployment scheduler is aimed to provide the mass deployment functionality which will be based on release and feature status as optional where we can select transports based on a deployment plan and the target system for the deployment.
  • SAP doesn’t have any plans for custom statuses in change management, but workflow API is planned for Q1 2025, where we will be able to trigger an external workflow. SAP is also looking into providing a custom field in future so that at least this sub-status or this sub-workflow status can be shown in Cloud ALM in the feature. But it is not planned to change the main status screen of the feature to add some custom status.
  • To map a four-tier landscape in Cloud ALM SAP recommends looking into the workflow APIs as soon as they are available. For now, we can use the successfully tested status to do the checks in the QA and with the deployment scheduler feature we can have option to deploy the changes to the pre-production system. And then we can use the same approval as well for the production, so the ready for production status to render approval on the pre-prod and production level.

Current Workflow of change management in Cloud ALM

 

*Use Clean core tags to make the feature for clean core adaption.

Session 5 - Operational Excellence with SAP Cloud ALM: Driving the Clean Core Initiative – 3rd December 2024

How SAP Cloud ALM supports clean core approach adoption in Operations with its 5 principles

  • Processes -Business Process Monitoring and Job and Automation Monitoring processes are running, and we need to monitor processes and several KPIs which are actually monitored by the business process monitoring application.
  • Extensibility -   To drive business continuity of extensions for the Clean Core adoption is that when we try to do modifications and extensions in S4 system directly. SAP recommends not to modify too much as makes it easier to upgrade the systems.
  • Data – The data is exchanged between different Cloud solutions, if we consider master data, we need to ensure that this data is exchanged between the process and is running as expected. The integration monitoring application is developed to start with message-based monitoring more at technical level, and we cover events as data exchange at different technologies. SAP is now trying to bring a kind of a business object view in these exchange processes to support the data dimension of clean core.
  • Integration - Integration and exception monitoring is an entire application dedicated to the monitoring and supporting of   all type of integration artifacts from S/4 HANA, CPI, master data integration. We need to make sure that integration and data exchange processes are monitored and is happening in a reliable way. This helps in visualizing the message exchange between the systems with the status of the messages, so that we can understand if there is a problem in the integration between your systems to keep the integration clean
  •  Operations – To have a holistic and secure application and business process operations, SAP is planning to have operations as to help the customers understand how the landscape is running and how it can be optimized to have a clean system.

Clean Core Operation Dashboards Overview

There are multiple dashboards planned to give a deep dive the possibility to evaluate the quality of operations and take measures accordingly to optimize the solution for a clean core adaption.

 Operations Dashboards preview for Clean Core project

 

 

New & Planned offerings on the Cloud ALM Roadmap for clean core adoption

 

Planned Future Roadmap to enhance Cloud ALM capability

 

Link to Roadmap explorer : SAP Road Map Explorer

Key takeaways: ​SAP Cloud ALM for clean core adaption

  • A clean core helps customers to stay current and agile compliant with innovation from SAP
  • Clean core is a journey, which needs to be adapted by following a step-by-step approach
  • SAP Cloud ALM provides contribution for all five dimensions (processes, extensibility, data, integration, operations) of SAP’s Clean Core initiative.
  • Customers can build custom processes and functionality using side-by-side or built in extensions
  • Change Management in Cloud ALM is planned to be enhanced by Q4 2024 / Q1 2025
  • End to end monitoring drives business continuity and quality of process execution
  • SAP Cloud ALM operations will become the platform for clean core dashboards.
  •  SAP plans to provide operation status as well as improvement dashboards covering business service availability, system operations, application operations as well as business process operations.
  • SAP plans to provide security status as well as improvement dashboards covering security configuration as well as security runtime aspects.

Thank you for reading the blog, I hope this helps you find a summary on Clean Core Webinars.

Note: Some pictures have been taken from the slides shared by SAP for the webinar series.

entry.title: Refactoring Gaps in a Legacy Electric Information Design
entry.content:
Looking at labels on an electric panel can be one of the joys and frustrations, whether during house-hunting, routine maintenance, or an on-the job, paid or unpaid task when you encounter what the past has provided. If your predecessor was neat and tidy, you feel relaxed. If you see, or can't quite see, scrawly crayon-level content, your spider-senses could tingle. And you hope that's all that tingles!

My quest in this case was to digitize the known data, then improve the situation by building a user-friendly small app to retain institutional knowledge and hopefully produce a wiring diagram by filling in the logic between the 2 given data points. One was the panel switch detail, and the other was the circuit notes on outlets and switches from a prior survey. In an enterprise situation, this could be office power and network cabling, virtual appliance connectivity, or other places where you have an "as-built" situation that could improved.

A basic need was to keep the right light bulbs at hand (insert "how many programmers does it take to screw in a light bulb joke"), which would be simple except for 50 years of tech changes getting rid of incandescent and going almost all the way to smart bulbs.

Because I wanted to put rigor into the design but avoid writing obscure buried spreadsheet code, I went with as simple an SQL database design as I thought sufficient. Maybe I could cut an object or two if I refactor again. The 11 tables shown on the post cover are more like a handful of those things you find around the house: switches, outlets, fixtures, sockets, and in some cases, switch gangs. Here's where arbitrary decisions are made that can cause surprises later, such as picking a type for a key field (integer or string, i.e.), limiting character field lengths, and not knowing what is unique in practice and what real data will invalidate your design assumptions when your vaunted plan hits the field.

Starting point "A": labels on source

Starting point "B": labels on destination

One receptacle and two switches (with sensors and buttons).

The breaker labels on the panel itself are almost nil; I point out switch position 23 and note it has a label that seems to say "23" except there are 2 switches stuck into that opening. If the supposition was the panel numbers have a 1:1 relationship with circuits, that is wrong. We are outside the box design already, and need to account for position 23 incorporating 2 circuits not one. The other design revelation is that, while not shown here, other switches might cover 2 (or more) panel openings for those higher amperage circuits such as electric heating units might use. I'll add a "left/right" or "top/bottom" designator for the split fields, and keep track of any spanning multiple openings.

The working assumption now is that the labels on end-devices are valid, until proven incorrect. Perhaps the field survey identified the correct line, except meanwhile someone inadvertently took off cover plates and mixed up the labels. Small chance, but not small enough to wager a shocking surprise.

After deciding the design should differentiate various appliances (receptacles differ from bulb sockets, e.g.), I came up with basic inventory tables, and a few check tables. I did field work looking at which fixtures had multiple lamps on one switch, and which ones might be hardwired, timer or sensor-activated, etc. While foreign key checks can be done in a myriad of ways, I chose a few values that used tables over SQL statement constraints. Thus, I have "socket types" and "bulb types". The former is critical to quality, as a pin-end fluorescent tube won't screw in an Edison socket. The latter is more end user experience, such as brightness and color temperature. And a bit of budget consciousness as fancier implies costlier.

Would "LED" or "CFL" be required values for bulb type? While a string of bulbs might contain a mixture for forms (globes, spirals, funnels) and types, for appearance sake one might require all of the same kind. It might show up that way in an app, and users could be disillusioned when the real world can't be captured correctly.

Back to user requirements for a bit. If a detective knows their material, producing a design that accommodates users needs can be easily done. However, unlike the fictional Columbo who was one set ahead and had "one more question" for the accused, users turn the table once you think you understand the scope, by asking their own "one more thing." Listen closely, as those histories retold may contain a seed that unfurls into an entire set of requirements that adjust the scope like a gale force flaps your sails.

A primary goal in this particular project was to create end user reports in PDF form that could be shared electronically or printed for archive. And, adding fillable forms which would work either online or via hard-copy to get revised data back into the repository.  In a prior project, I worked with an ODBC connection to Microsoft SQL*Server tables, embedding a host of content into a PDF using Python and free modules. In this project, I kept the Python core but switched databases and database drivers. Typically, I would depend on Perl with well-tested DBI and DBD modules if I never wanted to share my code with anyone else. For a collaboration, I can say Python code is easier to describe. Fixing something up for a different task, after roughly 5 years on the shelf, is not to be taken lightly.

If each panel opening had one and only one switch, the primary key would just be the panel ID and slot number. To allow for splits, I added a third column.

Panel and branch

Because of the preexisting taxonomy terms (like stock or IPC codes), I used circuit_id instead of the panel keys; this also makes it flexible should a branch feed be redirected to another source (say, a solar-cell array). Then, on the sink or destination side, wall switch details and hopefully a forward-looking design that permits conversion to smart(er) grid controls, power-consumption metrics, and, well, keeps the lights on.

Documenting the "old" along with the "new" goes hand-in-glove with creating data structures and test/sample data. If you look at a legacy system to absorb the processes into a larger system, the data types and content may be more obvious than the process logic. It might be moot to ask "why did they wire it this way?" except there are customs and traditions in all stacks of life.

Adding art to the page is a documentation style, writ large by IKEA. I kept my plans there to a doable minimum, sketching up a few variations in black-and-white iconography to represent this multi-table answer to the "what kind of bulb" question?

Bulb, socket types

Table 1.

 

wxYZ

Next steps

I shared a fairly small-in-scope project, to upgrade information access on internal building systems, as an example of studying a situation to improve without impairing. I tried not to over-engineer the solution, adding features and documentation plus a road-map of operation/maintenance tasks. With responsibility comes freedom in this case.

Notes

Software libraries for this project

from reportlab.pdfgen import canvas
from reportlab.pdfbase import pdfform
from reportlab.lib.colors import Color, black, magenta, pink, blue, green, red
from reportlab.lib.pagesizes import letter, legal
import psycopg2

The last time around that hamster wheel I used Python 2.x, and meanwhile that's out of favor so the 3.x version must be at least:

#!/usr/bin/env python3.11

 Stay tuned if this works in a year much less 5.

entry.title: Re: How to trigger IDOC based on status change on Materia...
entry.content:
Just a brain-dump: You could set up a DRF (Data Replication Framework) replication based on communication scenario SAP_COM_0009.

In the filter definition (separate step from creating the communication arrangement), you can then define filter to only include business objects with "cross-plant material status" 71 or 72.

This would then trigger replication whenever a product with these status is created/changed (so not only when the status 71/72 is first set).

entry.title: Re: SAP Approuter Headers using Private Link Service for Azure Blob Storage
entry.content: Thanks for your response. The reason to use an Approuter is, that as far as i know SAP Cloud Integration (resp. Integration Suite) does not support Private Link Services directly. The App router acts as a proxy as described here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-private-link-service-use-cases-for-sap-cloud-integration-and-sap-build/ba-p/13542464 (see section "How to achieve this"). As this blog post is from 2 years ago, this might be outdated but I am not aware of a way to connect PLS with Integration Suite directly. In my opinion this is not an authentication issue, but rather a missing feature of the approuter.

entry.title: Do you know the different extensibility approaches for SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Enhancing Key User & Side-by-Side Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA learning journey.

Overview 
This LJ covers two important extensibility approaches for SAP S/4HANA that are available to customers and partners:

  • Key-User extensibility focuses on adaptations created by key users that have a deep business knowledge. Key-User extensibility comprises adding new fields or custom logic, adapting the standard UIs, creating custom UIs, and creating custom business objects.
  • Side-by-side extensibility on SAP Business Technology Platform allows developers to build their own applications, enhancing and supplementing existing SAP S/4HANA (on premise) apps and SAP S/4HANA Cloud apps.

Learning objectives

  • Get to know about the extensibility concept in SAP S/4HANA
  • Personalize the SAP Fiori launchpad
  • Manage the SAP Fiori launchpad content
  • Extend the SAP Fiori launchpad
  • Use variant management
  • Use SAP Fiori runtime authoring
  • Create data source extensions
  • Create custom fields
  • Enable custom database fields for usage in SAP Fiori applications
  • Create custom logic
  • Create custom business objects
  • Create a UI for a custom business object
  • Add custom logic to a custom business object
  • Create custom libraries and methods
  • Create custom code lists
  • Transport extension items
  • Describe essential features of side-by-side extensions
  • Name the most important factors that influence the choice of the extensibility option
  • Develop an extension for SAP S/4HANA with SAP BTP ABAP Environment

Prerequisites

  • Introduction to Application Programming on SAP 

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margi

entry.title: Re: How to derive default cost center in the payment allo...
entry.content:
Cost center id shows incase of Allocation to G/L Account in the payment allocation or else, Profit center(Cash) derivation for journal entry will depend on applied Reporting Principle. 

Note that, even though you assign the invoice directly in the bank statement creation, it's not the bank statement itself that clears the invoice, but a clearing document resulting from the payment allocation of that bank statement item. Therefore, the bank statement itself has no cost object (like Cost Center, Material, etc) and since there is no reference of base profit center, the line doesn't inherit the profit center for the bank statement journal entry or derived from default profit center.

entry.title: Re: What are the options for restoring a project version...
entry.content:
I usually create a different project containing the decision rules and the process visiblity scenario's. 
In my automations I call them by using using the API. 

This way, I can always downgrade the Decision Rules project. 

I also started doing this for a different reason, I wanted to hand over the decision rules to business key users, so they could maintain the rules themselves. But I didn't want them to be able to edit the process and its automations itself. This is why I mostly have different projects for both. 

 

Kind regards

Jana

entry.title: Simplified sourcing for vendor selection in SAP HANA - Auto sourcing in PIR
entry.content:
Intro:

This blog concerns vendor simplified selection for Purchase requisition while running MRP in Hana. So it will be easy to convert the PR to a Purchase order. It would be helpful for customers moving from ECC to HANA.

Main Part:

MRP selects the source of supplies with the following priority:

  1. Quota – MEQ1 (Will be always considered in HANA)
  2. Source list – ME01 – If Multiple Schedule agreements and contracts are available, one vendor will be selected based on the sorting below.
  • priority ASCENDING: Logic shown at bottom
  • fixed_vendor DESCENDING: within a source list if 'fixed vendor is checked' then it is preferred
  • ebeln ASCENDING: Contract/Schedule agreement number with the smaller Document Number
  • ebelp ASCENDING: Contract/Schedule agreement line item with the smaller Document Item Number
  • lifnr ASCENDING: Source entry with the smaller Vendor/Supplier name
  • ekorg ASCENDING: Source entry with the smaller Purchasing Organization
  • esokz ASCENDING: Source entry with the suitable Special indicator (Purchasing info record category: Consignment/Subcontracting etc.)
  • ematn ASCENDING: Source entry with the smaller Manufacturer part number (MPN name)

If Multiple PIRs are available, one vendor will be selected based on the sorting below. (No need for active auto-sourcing check box)

  • Priority ASCENDING
  • Purchasing info record with the given plant
  • Purchasing info record with the smaller supplier number ASCENDING
  • Purchasing info record with the smaller purchasing organization ASCENDING
  • Purchasing info record with the smaller purchasing group ASCENDING (Even it will be picked from material master. don't know why added here - please comment)
  1. Schedule agreement – ME31L – If Multiple similar suppliers are maintained, then one vendor will be selected based on the sorting below.
  • priority ASCENDING
  • fixed_vendor DESCENDING <-- Within the source list if 'fixed vendor is checked' new criteria
  • ebeln ASCENDING <-- Schedule number
  • ebelp ASCENDING <-- Schedule line-item number
  • lifnr ASCENDING <-- Vendor/Supplier Name
  • ekorg ASCENDING <-- Purchase Organisation
  • esokz ASCENDING <-- Purchasing info record category
  • ematn ASCENDING <-- Manufacturer part number (MPN name)
  1. Contract – ME31K - If Multiple similar suppliers are maintained, then one vendor will be selected based on the sorting below.
  • priority ASCENDING
  • fixed_vendor DESCENDING <-- Within the source list if 'fixed vendor is checked' new criteria
  • ebeln ASCENDING <-- Contract number
  • ebelp ASCENDING <-- Contract line-item number
  • lifnr ASCENDING <-- Vendor/Supplier Name
  • ekorg ASCENDING <-- Purchase Organisation
  • esokz ASCENDING <-- Purchasing info record category
  • ematn ASCENDING <-- Manufacturer part number (MPN name)
  1. Purchasing info records with active auto-sourcing indicator - ME11 - If Multiple similar suppliers, then one vendor will be selected based on the sorting below.
  • Priority ASCENDING
  • Purchasing info record with the given plant
  • Purchasing info record with the smaller supplier number ASCENDING
  • Purchasing info record with the smaller purchasing organization ASCENDING
  • Purchasing info record with the smaller purchasing group ASCENDING

Note: When creating the source of supply data for a material (PIR, Contract) the system will set its priority in table CDS_M_PUR_SOS_B based on the criteria below.

Priority=1 for scheduling agreements with the given plant.
Priority=2 for scheduling agreements without given plant
Priority=3 for contracts with a given plant
Priority=4 for contracts without given plant
Priority=5 for contracts with the given plant but with reference org
Priority=6 for contracts without given plant but with reference org
Priority=11 for info record with a given plant
Priority=12 for info record without given plant

Test case: Let us test using the bottom-up approach.

  1. Multiple PIRs

Priority 12 - info record without given plant

I have created Multiple PIRs with the auto-sourcing flag active and maintained them at the client level without plant. In Background, the system will set the priority as 12 against each source line item in the table CDS_M_PUR_SOS_B

During the MRP run the system chooses only one supplier based on the below sorting procedure.

  • Priority ASCENDING – 12 (5 items will be selected)
  • Purchasing info record with the given plant – empty (5 items will be selected)
  • Purchasing info record with the smaller supplier number ASCENDING – 100000141 (2 items will be selected)
  • Purchasing info record with the smaller purchasing organization ASCENDING – 1010 (Vendor has been chosen)
  • Purchasing info record with the smaller purchasing group ASCENDING - 001

Priority 11 - info record with given plant

Now, additional PIRs have been created with Plant combinations, and in the background, the system sets the priority to 11 against each line item.

During MRP run the system chooses the supplier based on the sorting procedure.

  • Priority ASCENDING – 11 (4 items will be selected)
  • Purchasing info record with the given plant – 0075 (4 items will be selected)
  • Purchasing info record with the smaller supplier number ASCENDING – 100000142 (2 items will be selected)
  • Purchasing info record with the smaller purchasing organization ASCENDING – 1010 (Vendor has been chosen)
  • Purchasing info record with the smaller purchasing group ASCENDING - 001

  1. Multiple contracts

Priority 5 - contracts with a given plant but with reference org

I have created multiple contracts with reference purchase org, plant. In the background, the system has set priority 5 against respective line items. Auto sourcing is also activated while creating contracts and Sch agg.

During MRP the system uses the below sorting method to choose the vendor.

  • priority ASCENDING – 5 (2 items will be selected)
  • fixed_vendor DESCENDING <-- Within the source list if 'fixed vendor is checked' new criteria – Empty (2 items will be selected)
  • ebeln ASCENDING <-- Contract number – 4600000024 (1 item will be selected)
  • ebelp ASCENDING <-- Contract line-item number – 20 (1 item has been selected)
  • lifnr ASCENDING <-- Vendor/Supplier Name – 100000141 (Vendor has been chosen)
  • ekorg ASCENDING <-- Purchase Organisation - 0075
  • esokz ASCENDING <-- Purchasing info record category - K
  • ematn ASCENDING <-- Manufacturer part number (MPN name) – Empty

Priority=4 contracts without given plant

I have created multiple contracts with normal purchase org at the client level without a plant. In the background, the priority has been set to 4 against each line item.

During MRP the system uses the below sorting mentioned to choose the vendor.

  • priority ASCENDING – 4 (4 items will be selected)
  • fixed_vendor DESCENDING <-- Within the source list if 'fixed vendor is checked' new criteria – Empty (4 items will be selected)
  • ebeln ASCENDING <-- Contract number – 4600000023 (4 items will be selected & sorted)
  • ebelp ASCENDING <-- Contract line-item number – 10 (1 item will be selected)
  • lifnr ASCENDING <-- Vendor/Supplier Name – 100000142 (Vendor has been chosen)
  • ekorg ASCENDING <-- Purchase Organisation - 0075
  • esokz ASCENDING <-- Purchasing info record category - Empty
  • ematn ASCENDING <-- Manufacturer part number (MPN name) – Empty

Priority=3 contracts with a given plant

I have created multiple contracts with normal purchase org at the Plant level. In the background, the priority has been set to 3 against each line item.

During the MRP run the system uses the below sorting method to choose the vendor.

  • priority ASCENDING – 3 (2 line items will be selected)
  • fixed_vendor DESCENDING <-- Within the source list if 'fixed vendor is checked' new criteria – Empty (2 line items will be selected)
  • ebeln ASCENDING <-- Contract number – 4600000028 (1 line item will be selected & sorted)
  • ebelp ASCENDING <-- Contract line-item number – 10 (1 line item will be selected & sorted)
  • lifnr ASCENDING <-- Vendor/Supplier Name – 100000140 (Vendor has been chosen)
  • ekorg ASCENDING <-- Purchase Organisation - 0075
  • esokz ASCENDING <-- Purchasing info record category - Empty
  • ematn ASCENDING <-- Manufacturer part number (MPN name) – Empty

  1. Multiple Scheduling agreement

Priority=1 scheduling agreements with the given plant.

I have created multiple schedule agreements with normal purchase org at the Plant level. In the background, the priority has been set to 1 against each line item.

During MRP the system uses the below sorting method to choose the vendor.

  • priority ASCENDING – 1 (4 line items will be selected)
  • fixed_vendor DESCENDING <-- Within the source list if 'fixed vendor is checked' new criteria – Empty (4 line items will be selected)
  • ebeln ASCENDING <-- Schedule number – 5500000008 (1 line item will be selected based on sorting)
  • ebelp ASCENDING <-- Schedule line-item number – 10 (1 line item will be selected)
  • lifnr ASCENDING <-- Vendor/Supplier Name – 10000141 (Vendor has been chosen)
  • ekorg ASCENDING <-- Purchase Organisation – DE01
  • esokz ASCENDING <-- Purchasing info record category - L
  • ematn ASCENDING <-- Manufacturer part number (MPN name) - Empty

You see the auto-sourcing is useful only at priority level 12.

  1. Source list – We have multiple combinations here

Case 3: Source list with scheduling agreement (55*), contract (46*), and PIR.

Here the Scheduling agreement takes the lead.

Even though we had PIR and Contract (46**) the system considered only the Scheduling agreement (55**) from the source list and chose the vendor assigned to it during the MRP.

As the source list contains multiple sch. agg the sorting method below will be used to choose the vendor.

  • priority ASCENDING – 1 (3 items will be selected)
  • fixed_vendor DESCENDING <-- Within the source list if 'fixed vendor is checked' new criteria – Empty (3 items will be selected)
  • ebeln ASCENDING <-- Schedule number in source list – 5500000008 (1 item will be selected via sorting)
  • ebelp ASCENDING <-- Schedule line-item number - 10
  • lifnr ASCENDING <-- Vendor/Supplier Name – 10000141 (Vendor has been chosen)
  • ekorg ASCENDING <-- Purchase Organisation – DE01
  • esokz ASCENDING <-- Purchasing info record category - L
  • ematn ASCENDING <-- Manufacturer part number (MPN name) Empty

Case 2: Source list with multiple Contracts (46*) and PIR.

During MRP the system will give priority to the contract assigned to the source list and pick the vendor against it.

The sorting method below is used to choose a vendor if multiple contracts are there in the source list

  • priority ASCENDING – 3 (2 items will be selected)
  • fixed_vendor DESCENDING <-- Within the source list if 'fixed vendor is checked' new criteria – Empty (2 items will be selected)
  • ebeln ASCENDING <-- Contract number – 4600000028 (1 item will be selected via sorting)
  • ebelp ASCENDING <-- Contract line-item number - 10
  • lifnr ASCENDING <-- Vendor/Supplier Name – 100000140 (Vendor has been chosen)
  • ekorg ASCENDING <-- Purchase Organisation - 0075
  • esokz ASCENDING <-- Purchasing info record category - Empty
  • ematn ASCENDING <-- Manufacturer part number (MPN name) – Empty

Case 1: Source list with only PIR

Here PIR takes the lead as there are no Sch agg and Contract. Also you can see auto sourcing is unchecked.

If the source list contains multiple PIRs, then the selection criteria below will be used to choose the vendor. The auto-sourcing flag does not affect this.

  • Priority ASCENDING – 11 (8 items have been selected)
  • Purchasing info record with the given plant – 0075 (8 items have been selected & sorted)
  • Purchasing info record with the smaller supplier number ASCENDING – 100000140 (2 items have been selected & sorted)
  • Purchasing info record with the smaller purchasing organization ASCENDING – DE01 (Vendor selected) But
  • Purchasing info record with the smaller purchasing group ASCENDING – 001

Here Purchasing org 1010 is selected but in the source list, DE01 is maintained as relevant to MRP. Need to check with SAP.

No similar vendors can be set relevant to MRP in the source list.

  1. Quota

I have created a quota.

During MRP the system directly picks the relevant supplier based on quota.

As the priority increases with high-priority master data, the PIR with less priority does not affect MRP.

Prerequisites for quota: Splitting quota to be activated in lot sizing procedure and assigned to Material.

SIMG - Production - Material Requirements Planning - Planning – Lot Size Calculation - Check Lot Sizing Procedure

Conclusion:

I hope this may help you to know how the vendor is getting selected in HANA MRP after simplification. Comment if there are errors. Please have look to 2614920.

entry.title: Re: Applying the pipeline concept for a point-to-point integration scenario
entry.content:
Thanks @alex_bundschuh  for your swift response.

We just having a query on using the same JMS queue for different senders as if one flood the queue by sending data more than 300MB then the msgs from sender2 might not be processed or can be lost

And thus we want to have separate queue for easier maintenance. Or what would you suggest the better option to adapt during high throughput of msgs via the JMS queue. If both the senders are passing the data , whether the step06 of generic outbound processing could able to seamlessly process the messages from the same JMS queue PIPQ04? Would it cause any performance impacts?

entry.title: How Gen AI can supercharge the quality of your ERP migration
entry.content:
Our research[1] shows that over 93% of organizations that haven’t upgraded to SAP S/4HANA have already started or plan to migrate within the next 24 months. Yet these organizations will naturally face a number of transitional challenges – ranging from security risks and lack of in-house expertise to customer dissatisfaction and possible financial impact. In order to succeed, organizations must implement robust continuous testing methods that will minimize this risk and deliver uninterrupted business outcomes, while managing the additional changes and updates demanded after the transition. Technology will inevitably play a critical role in executing this strategy effectively, and the rise of new technologies - like Generative AI - must be embraced in future approaches. Read more on how GenAI can aid this transformation here https://bit.ly/3YTE1OH

[1] State of Worldwide Business Assurance for SAP Solutions 2023

entry.title: Role restriction for incident depending on the incident category
entry.content:
Greetings Community,

We have a request from a client who wishes to restrict certain roles in the incident reporting system to only allow the submission of Safety Observations and Near Misses.

This means that users assigned to these roles should not be able to report an incident.

We understand that there is a specific object for incident categories (INC_CATEG). However, after changing the settings to include only Near Misses and Safety Observations, as shown in the attached screenshot, we found that users with the restricted role are still able to create incidents, indicating that the restrictions are not being enforced. 

Could anyone provide insights on what might be causing this issue or offer recommendations on how to resolve it?

Besides, if we want to make restrictions with the ID of locations, they do not show up in the object. is there a solution to this?

 

Thank you in advance for your assistance.

entry.title: SAP ABAP SDK for IBM watsonx v1.2.0 released
entry.content:
On March 28, 2024, IBM had published the first release of its ABAP® Software Development Kit (SDK) for IBM® watsonx™ services. The watsonx.ai service can be used to train, validate, tune, and deploy foundation and machine learning models. The watsonx.ai Runtime, formerly known as Watson Machine Learning, is used to build, run and manage generative AI and machine learning applications with built-in performance and scalability.

The ABAP SDK provides a class library that can be downloaded to SAP systems using abapGit and comes in two flavors:

  1. ABAP SDK for IBM watsonx, using SAP NetWeaver for the SAP Business Suite running on SAP NetWeaver 7.50 or above and for the SAP S/4HANA on-premises edition.
  2. ABAP SDK for IBM watsonx, using SAP BTP ABAP Environment for the SAP Business Technology Platform ABAP Environment.

On December 3, 2024, version 1.2.0 of the SDK was released by IBM. Enhancements in this release include new watsonx.ai methods in the groups Text Chat, Text Extraction and Text Rerank. More information about the changes in version 1.2.0 can be found in the repositories in the migration document MIGRATION-V1.2.0.md.

Detailed information on how to setup and use the SDK can be found in the README.md files of the GitHub repositories. The README.md files also provide two comprehensive examples to show how the supported services can be used. An even more elaborated article about how use watsonx services with the ABAP SDK can be found in Joachim Rese's blog article How to Bring Generative AI to Your ABAP Program Using the ABAP SDK for IBM watsonx.

entry.title: move & move back of table changes order of objects => how to rearrange?
entry.content:
Hi,
in a tabstrip there is 1 table (always visible) and 1 listbox which is invisible at start and can be made visible by clicking a button. As the listbox should always be above the table its background colour is white and on the top of the Tab_2 in the overview:


if a user wants to edit the table with the help of builder panel the table is moved to another tab. After editing is finished the table is moved back to its original postion and this is where my problem emerges:
when the listbox is made visible now it is barely readable because the table is on top.
I have not found an API to directly alter order of objets on a page like in the builder:
any suggestions/workarounds to push the listbox to the top again?
my current workaround: when the table is moved back I simply move and re-move the listbox too. By far not optimal but its working for now.
thank you & br
edgar

entry.title: Settlement management - incorrect cancellation of accruals
entry.content:
I have Settlement Management set up with settings very close to standard and I have the following issue. 

There are 3 settlement dates: 1 - Delta accrual, 2 - Delta accrual, 3 - Partial settlement (and this goes on as accruals are posted every month and partial settlement every quarter). 
1  posts the accrual OK, 252 USD - correct - both RES1 and RED1 condition populated, but only RES1 active
2 cancels 1, then post, 630 USD - correct - - both RES1 and RED1 condition populated, but only RES1 active. REA2 is there as cancellation with -252 (correct)
3 (partial settlement) cancels both 1 and 2 with REA2 -882 which should only be -630, then posts full quarter amount (correctly). Here, accrual 1 should not be cancelled again, only accrual 2. 
How can this be fixed?

entry.title: Re: New Pipeline Concept features: new partner id definition, alternative partner, bypass option
entry.content:
@webwolf I wouldn't go for a concatenated combination of sender and message type, one of the reasons for using alternative partners is to overcome the partner id lengths restrictions, especially for IDoc message types as a combination of basic type, message type and potential extension to easily reach the upper length limit.

So, one option to support your use case would be to define different partner IDs but then in the interface determination you can actually point to the very same endpoint of your scenario-specific outbound processing flow. Drawback is that if the endpoint changes you need to do redundant changes, also initial maintenance effort is higher.

I looked into options to enhance the pipeline concept to support this wildcard use case. If we simply passed the partner ID from the scenario-specific integration flow to the first generic inbound processing flow then there was no need to determine a partner ID and I would be more flexible in defining such sort of wildcard scenarios. As an outlook, for the next version I have enhanced the groovy script accordingly, if the partner ID exists already then I do not read it from the Partner Directory.

The issue in your case is that for IDocs the first generic flow is actually the generic IDoc inbound, since this is generic I am not able to set a partner ID, so the only option is to read the partner ID based on the incoming sender and interface. So, I thought about the option to add more alternative partners using additional schemes SenderInterface_1, SenderInterface_2, etc. and then I would loop over a number of sender interfaces, I would only loop if there is no hit for the SenderInterface scheme, and I need to define an upper boundary, say 20 loops at most. The disadvantages are obvious, you may have a runtime impact and with an upper limit you are not really flexible, what if you need to define 21 interfaces, though this upper limit might be customizable. What do you think, would this approach be acceptable for your use case?

Alex

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Recife 2025
entry.content:
Já temos data para mais uma edição do SAP Inside Track Recife 2025 e reunir a Comunidade SAP para compartilhar conhecimento, estreitar laços profissionais e explorar as últimas inovações no universo SAP.

Veja como foi a ultima edição: Blog SITREC2024

Acompanhe palestras anteriores aqui neste link: SAP Inside Track Recife - Playlist

A agenda do evento 2025 ainda não foi divulgada.

 

Um Pouco da Nossa História

O SAP Inside Track teve sua primeira edição em Recife em 2016 e, desde então, tem sido um marco importante para a comunidade SAP na região. O evento faz parte de uma série global presente em muitos países, sendo organizado por membros ativos da Comunidade SAP com o intuito de promover o compartilhamento de conhecimento e fortalecer conexões profissionais.

No Brasil, o SAP Inside Track já acumula mais de 15 anos de existência, passando por 11 cidades diferentes em 40 edições pelo país. Cidades como São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro, São Leopoldo, Joinville, Campinas, Salvador, Ribeirão Preto, Belo Horizonte, Curitiba, Fortaleza e, claro, Recife, já receberam o evento.

O Que Esperar do Evento

O SAP Inside Track Recife é a maior oportunidade de aprender, compartilhar experiências e conhecer outros profissionais do mercado SAP. Teremos palestras técnicas, funcionais e de negócios, abordando as mais diversas soluções SAP, incluindo as inovações do SAP S/4HANA.

Além disso, este é o momento perfeito para expandir seu networking e conhecer pessoalmente grandes profissionais da Comunidade SAP e das redes sociais.

Veja como foram as edições passadas neste link -> #SITREC

Data: 17/05/2025

Local: A definir

Inscrição: Não liberada (Inscrições para aquisição de ingressos no Eventbrite)

Chamada para Palestrantes

Se você tem conhecimento para compartilhar, esta é sua oportunidade! Envie sua proposta com o tema, título e uma sinopse para: sapinsidetrack@gmail.com. O tempo de palestra é de no máximo 40 minutos, e o último dia para envio de propostas é em 05 de Agosto de 2024. Aguarde o contato da nossa equipe com a confirmação.

Agenda: A definir

 

Sobre Recife

Recife, a capital do estado de Pernambuco, é uma das cidades mais antigas do Brasil e a quarta mais desenvolvida entre os mercados emergentes do mundo. Destaca-se pelo maior parque tecnológico do Brasil, o Porto Digital, e pelo segundo maior polo médico do país. Com um aeroporto premiado e uma rica cultura gastronômica, Recife é um polo turístico e de negócios ideal para sediar eventos nacionais e internacionais, graças à sua infraestrutura de ponta e à vibrante cena cultural e tecnológica.

Organizadores e Patrocínio

O SAP Inside Track Recife é organizado por membros dedicados da Comunidade SAP, com apoio de diversos patrocinadores que tornam este evento possível. Gostaríamos de agradecer a todos que contribuem para o sucesso deste encontro.

Apoio: A definir

Junte-se a nós no SAP Inside Track Recife 2025!

Esperamos vê-lo lá para um dia repleto de aprendizado, networking e inovação.

entry.title: Re: Export multiple accesses data of a user on a course
entry.content: @nicolasanni, the "PH_STUD_CPNT" table could be tried via PRD. To compare the results you get from PRD, the "User Learning Plan Audit Report (CSV)" could be run - it stands to use the same table. From the "User Learning Plan Audit Report (CSV)", the "Last Updated By" column, where the user itself appears (User - ID) and the "Action" one, where "Modify" is the entry, would be related to times the course was opened by the user.

entry.title: Re: Splitting a string with a delimiter in CPI using groovy script
entry.content: @Dagmar first you need to get the substring of the first 10 chars to have the entire date then split that text by the dash and finally compose your ISO date. String sample = "2024-12-04 07:09 UTC"; def parts = sample[0..10].split("-").collect{it as String}; String date_in_ISO = parts[0] + parts[1] + parts[2]; println(date_in_ISO);
